sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Introduce an info action sketching basic ideas for reforming Cardano treasury governance and bringing them into public discussion.
Stated amount: not stated in document

Deliverables: Basic propositions for treasury governance reform, Proposed treasury governance voting sequence, Proposed thematic budget structure, Call for ecosystem collaboration on further refinements and implementation

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal says Cardano treasury governance is currently in a poor state due to a funding impasse and insufficient direct support from the existing governance system.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal claims treasury withdrawal actions from different important domains have created perceived competition for scarce resources and contributed to hostility, toxicity, ill will, and strife in the community.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal argues treasury governance must provide clear direction, a coherent strategy, and a specific roadmap for Cardano's growth and stability.
Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal claims treasury governance should be explicitly addressed and supported by the Cardano governance system and constitution.
Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposal argues the NCL should not be treated as the overall treasury budget and is only a vague initial approximation for budgeting.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal calls for dedicated and balanced budgets with thematic domains rather than isolated treasury withdrawals for individual projects being voted against one another.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposal says Cardano treasury governance is currently in a poor state due to a funding impasse and insufficient direct support from the existing governance system.

Independent evidence for: The proposal claims treasury withdrawal actions from different important domains have created perceived competition for scarce resources and contributed to hostility, toxicity, ill will, and strife in the community.
Independent evidence for: The proposal argues the NCL should not be treated as the overall treasury budget and is only a vague initial approximation for budgeting.

Independent evidence for: The proposal asserts DReps should vote on budgets instead of individual project funding because directly comparing individual proposals is cognitively inefficient and requires domain expertise most DReps lack.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal argues treasury governance must provide clear direction, a coherent strategy, and a specific roadmap for Cardano's growth and stability." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposal says Cardano treasury governance is currently in a poor state due to a funding impasse and insufficient direct support from the existing governance system." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
